opportunities.title: FEMA — Building Resilient Infrastructure and Communities (BRIC) FY 2024–2025
opportunities.one_liner: A $1 billion federal competitive grant program funding shovel-ready infrastructure projects that reduce flood and hazard risk in communities.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: FEMA has published the FY 2024 and 2025 BRIC funding opportunity, making $1 billion in federal funding available to states, local governments, territories, and Tribal Nations to take proactive steps to protect communities from disasters including floods. BRIC provides funding for public infrastructure projects and mitigation efforts that bolster a community's flood resilience before a disaster strikes, with a special emphasis on shovel-ready construction projects or adoption of hazard-resistant building codes. Federal funding covers up to 75 percent of eligible activity cost, and small, impoverished communities may be eligible for up to a 90 percent federal cost share. Applicants and subapplicants must have a FEMA-approved hazard mitigation plan by the application deadline.
opportunities.eligibility: States, local governments, territories, Tribal Nations (via FEMA GO portal)
opportunities.budget: $1 billion total; max $20M federal share per project (National Competition)

opportunities.title: FEMA — Flood Mitigation Assistance (FMA) FY 2024
opportunities.one_liner: A $600 million competitive grant specifically targeting flood risk reduction for NFIP-insured properties through acquisition, elevation, and flood-proofing projects.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: For fiscal year 2024, up to $600 million is available to states, local communities, Tribal Nations, and territories to reduce or eliminate the risk of repetitive flood damage to buildings insured under the National Flood Insurance Program. FEMA published an amendment to the funding opportunity on April 30, 2026, incorporating recent federal guidance; funding comes from the National Flood Insurance Fund and the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act. The goal of the FMA grant program is to employ cost-effective measures to reduce or eliminate the long-term risk of flood damage to buildings, manufactured homes, and other structures insured under the NFIP. Applicants have until 3 p.m. ET on August 6, 2026, to submit or revise and re-submit their application to FEMA.
opportunities.eligibility: States, territories, local governments, federally recognized Tribal Nations

opportunities.title: NFWF — National Coastal Resilience Fund (NCRF) 2026
opportunities.one_liner: A NOAA-backed foundation grant funding nature-based solutions to protect coastal communities from storms, flooding, and erosion, with awards up to $7 million.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: The National Coastal Resilience Fund (NCRF), established in 2018, invests in nature-based solutions that protect coastal communities while enhancing habitats for fish and wildlife, including coastal marshes, wetlands, dune and beach systems, oyster and coral reefs, rivers, and floodplains. NFWF has closed pre-proposals for its 2026 NCRF and is moving selected applicants into the full proposal phase; full proposals are due June 24, 2026, with awards announced November–December 2026. Implementation awards typically range from $1 million to $7 million, while planning and design awards range from $100,000 to $1.5 million. Eligible applicants include 501(c) nonprofits, state and local governments, tribal governments and organizations, educational institutions, and commercial entities in US coastal and Great Lakes states and territories.
opportunities.eligibility: Nonprofits, state/local/tribal governments, universities, commercial entities; US coastal/Great Lakes states and territories; invite-only after pre-proposal stage

opportunities.title: EPA — Sewer Overflow and Stormwater Reuse Municipal Grants (OSG)
opportunities.one_liner: Rolling federal grants to states for green and gray stormwater infrastructure projects that reduce flooding and improve water quality in communities.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: The EPA's Sewer Overflow and Stormwater Reuse Municipal Grants Program helps communities and states reduce water pollution caused by stormwater and sewer runoff, funding green and gray infrastructure projects that strengthen stormwater collection systems, improve flood resilience, and address water quality issues. EPA announced $52 million in grants to help communities improve essential stormwater infrastructure, implementing new requirements under the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law to help states support projects in small and financially distressed communities. The OSG program is administered by states and deadlines may differ; applicants should approach their state Clean Water State Revolving Fund Program or EPA Regional Office for specific deadlines.
opportunities.eligibility: Municipalities and local governments (via state programs)

opportunities.title: NSF — Confronting Hazards, Impacts and Risks for a Resilient Planet (CHIRRP)
opportunities.one_liner: An NSF standing research program funding transformative Earth system science on hazard knowledge and risk mitigation, including flood and coastal hazards, in partnership with communities.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: The CHIRRP program invites projects focusing on innovative and transformative research that advances Earth system hazard knowledge and risk mitigation in partnership with affected communities, addressing hazards compounded by changing climates, rising populations, and aging infrastructure. Resilience to natural hazards remains a high priority for NSF, which strongly encourages researchers to submit proposals for fundamental research that could yield insights useful to advance natural disaster resilience. NSF supports research into natural hazards such as hurricanes, floods, wildfires, coastal erosion, and severe thunderstorms, and methods to reduce their harmful impacts. This is a standing program accepting proposals on an ongoing basis through NSF's standard proposal submission process.
opportunities.eligibility: US universities, research institutions, community organizations
opportunities.budget: Varies per proposal (standard NSF research grants)

opportunities.title: UKRI / EPSRC — NetworkPlus in Property Flood Resilience
opportunities.funder: UKRI / Engineering and Physical Sciences Research Council (EPSRC)
opportunities.one_liner: A UK Research and Innovation award of up to £2.5 million to establish a research network advancing science and practice for property-level flood resilience.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: EPSRC will fund 80% of the full economic cost of up to £2.5 million for a duration of up to three years to establish a NetworkPlus focused on property-level flood resilience. The funding opportunity is invite-only; potential applicants were expected to attend a consortium building workshop on March 23, 2026, in Swindon to be invited to apply. The program is specifically focused on building a UK research community around property-level flood resilience measures, bridging gaps between research, policy, and practice. The funding opportunity was set to open in spring 2026, with more information available on the UKRI page.
opportunities.eligibility: UK research organisations (consortium required; invite-only after workshop attendance)
opportunities.budget: Up to £2.5 million (80% funded by EPSRC)

opportunities.title: Horizon Europe — HORIZON-MISS-2026-01-CLIMA-06: Improving Climate Resilience of Navigable Inland Waterways and Related Water Infrastructure
opportunities.funder: European Commission / Horizon Europe (EU Mission: Adaptation to Climate Change)
opportunities.one_liner: A €6 million Horizon Europe Mission grant to improve the flood and climate resilience of inland waterways through systemic nature-based and engineering solutions.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: This Horizon Europe funding call focuses on improving climate resilience of navigable inland waterways and related water infrastructure, where climate and environmental risks such as flooding and ecological degradation are addressed in a systemic way. Cost-efficient and environmentally friendly measures—especially nature-based solutions—are identified with stakeholder involvement to improve climate resilience while supporting integrated co-benefits. Applications are open to consortia with at least 3 participants from different countries based in EU Member States, Associated Countries, and low- and middle-income countries. This Research and Innovation Action provides up to €6 million for up to 5 years, with applications closing on September 23, 2026.
opportunities.eligibility: Consortia of ≥3 organisations from EU Member States/Associated Countries plus low- and middle-income countries

opportunities.title: Horizon Europe — Disaster Resilient Society for Europe (Cluster 3) 2026
opportunities.one_liner: A Horizon Europe Cluster 3 call targeting innovation for disaster-resilient European societies, including tools for flood risk awareness and disaster response, with proposals due November 2026.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: The Horizon Europe 2026 Disaster Resilient Society for Europe call has proposals due by November 5, 2026, covering five topics including innovative tools for search and rescue in hazardous conditions and driving innovation uptake of disaster risk solutions. One topic, HORIZON-CL3-2026-01-DRS-05, focuses on climate security and civil preparedness—developing new ways to create pre- and post-crisis climate-change-related scenarios for a more resilient Europe. This cluster funds applied research and innovation actions linking technology developers, civil protection agencies, and local authorities to develop and deploy flood and disaster resilience solutions at scale across Europe. Funding per project typically ranges from €4–10 million.
opportunities.eligibility: Consortia from EU Member States and Associated Countries

opportunities.title: Horizon Europe PCP WISE — Water Intelligence Products and Services Powered by Earth Observation
opportunities.funder: European Commission / Horizon Europe (PCP WISE consortium of 12 public buyers)
opportunities.one_liner: A pre-commercial procurement call co-funded by Horizon Europe to develop next-generation EO-powered flood monitoring and stormwater management tools.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: Launched on September 5, 2025, the PCP WISE group of 12 buyers issued a Call for Tenders to co-develop next-generation water intelligence products and services powered by Earth Observation, funded under Horizon Europe's Pre-Commercial Procurement mechanism. This three-phase PCP funds R&D services—from solution design to prototype development and field validation—targeting TRL 8 solutions that monitor the Soil-Water-Vegetation system and help predict, prevent, and manage water-related crises such as floods, fires, and droughts. One focus area is Urban Water Excess (East-North Europe), specifically targeting flood and stormwater management with real-time tools. Eligible participants include technology companies and research organisations from EU and associated countries.
opportunities.eligibility: Technology companies and research organisations from EU and associated countries
opportunities.budget: Multi-phase R&D contract funding (exact envelope per phase to be confirmed)

opportunities.title: EPA Sewer Overflow and Stormwater Reuse Municipal Grants (OSG Program)
opportunities.funder: U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), administered through states
opportunities.one_liner: EPA program funding green and gray infrastructure to reduce stormwater runoff, sewer overflows, and urban flooding.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: The EPA's Sewer Overflow and Stormwater Reuse Municipal Grants Program helps communities and states reduce water pollution caused by stormwater and sewer runoff, funding green and gray infrastructure projects that strengthen stormwater collection systems, improve flood resilience, and address water quality issues. The OSG program is administered by states and deadlines may differ; applicants should approach their state Clean Water State Revolving Fund program or EPA Regional Office for more information on specific deadlines. This program is particularly relevant for urban and pluvial flood risk reduction.

opportunities.title: EPA Water Infrastructure Finance and Innovation Act (WIFIA) Program
opportunities.one_liner: EPA's flexible, low-cost federal loan program for significant water and stormwater infrastructure projects that enhance community flood resilience.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: The Water Infrastructure Finance and Innovation Act (WIFIA) accelerates investment in water infrastructure by offering flexible financing for significant drinking water and wastewater infrastructure projects, providing long-term loans to help communities implement critical water infrastructure at lower cost. EPA's WIFIA program has announced $6.5 billion in WIFIA funding available for water systems, with an additional $550 million available to states through the State WIFIA (SWIFIA) program. Letters of Interest are accepted on a continuous, rolling basis. Projects eligible include stormwater runoff mitigation and flood-resilient water infrastructure.
opportunities.eligibility: Water utilities, municipalities, states, and other eligible water infrastructure project sponsors
opportunities.budget: $6.5 billion in WIFIA funding; additional $550 million via State WIFIA (SWIFIA)

opportunities.title: FEMA FY2026 Pre-Disaster Mitigation (PDM) Grant Program
opportunities.one_liner: FY2026 congressionally directed pre-disaster mitigation grants primarily focused on infrastructure improvements to reduce flooding risk.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: The FEMA Pre-Disaster Mitigation Grant Program funds Congressionally Directed Spending projects for state, local, tribal, and territorial governments, designed to implement cost-effective measures that reduce natural hazard risk to people and property and decrease reliance on federal funding for future disasters. In FY2026, the PDM Grant Program will award $189,713,659 to projects identified in the 2026 Department of Homeland Security Appropriations Act's Joint Explanatory Statement. The FY2026 PDM projects come from 40 states and one tribe, with most projects focused on improving infrastructure to reduce flooding risk.
opportunities.eligibility: State, local, tribal, and territorial (SLTT) governments identified in the FY2026 DHS Appropriations Act

opportunities.title: EU LIFE Programme Climate Change Adaptation Strand (2026 Call)
opportunities.one_liner: EU LIFE programme funding implementation-focused climate adaptation projects including nature-based flood risk solutions.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: The LIFE Climate Change Adaptation strand funds projects that strengthen resilience, reduce climate vulnerability, and support implementation of EU adaptation policies, with an indicative €28 million budget for 2026 that prioritises risk assessment tools, nature-based solutions, climate-proof infrastructure, and systemic policy integration. The strand supports practical, scalable solutions that improve resilience across sectors, ecosystems, and governance levels while contributing to a climate-resilient Europe by 2050. Projects must demonstrate measurable impact, avoid maladaptation, and align with the European Climate Law and EU Adaptation Strategy.
opportunities.eligibility: Public and private organisations from EU member states and associated countries
opportunities.budget: ~€28 million indicative budget for 2026 adaptation strand
